   127→	// ForcePlanTimestamp, if not nil, will force the "plantimestamp" function
   128→	// to return the given value instead of the time when the plan operation
   129→	// started.
   130→	//
   131→	// This is here only to allow producing fixed results for tests. Don't
   132→	// use it for main code.
   133→	ForcePlanTimestamp *time.Time
   134→
   135→	// Forget if set to true will cause the plan to forget all resources. This is
   136→	// only allowd in the context of a destroy plan.
   137→	Forget bool
   138→}
   139→
   140→// Plan generates an execution plan by comparing the given configuration
   141→// with the given previous run state.
   142→//
   143→// The given planning options allow control of various other details of the
   144→// planning process that are not represented directly in the configuration.
   145→// You can use terraform.DefaultPlanOpts to generate a normal plan with no
   146→// special options.
   147→//
   148→// If the returned diagnostics contains no errors then the returned plan is
   149→// applyable, although Terraform cannot guarantee that applying it will fully
   150→// succeed. If the returned diagnostics contains errors but this method
   151→// still returns a non-nil Plan then the plan describes the subset of actions
   152→// planned so far, which is not safe to apply but could potentially be used
   153→// by the UI layer to give extra context to support understanding of the
   154→// returned error messages.
   155→func (c *Context) Plan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   156→	plan, _, diags := c.PlanAndEval(config, prevRunState, opts)
   157→	return plan, diags
   158→}
   159→
   160→// PlanAndEval is like [Context.Plan] except that it additionally makes a
   161→// best effort to return a [lang.Scope] which can evaluate expressions in the
   162→// root module based on the content of the generated plan.
   163→//
   164→// The scope will be nil if the planning process doesn't complete successfully
   165→// enough to produce a valid evaluation scope. If the returned plan is nil
   166→// then the scope will always be nil, but it's also possible for the scope
   167→// to be nil even when the plan isn't, if the plan is not complete enough for
   168→// the evaluation scope to produce consistent results.
   169→func (c *Context) PlanAndEval(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   170→	defer c.acquireRun("plan")()
   171→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   172→
   173→	// Save the downstream functions from needing to deal with these broken situations.
   174→	// No real callers should rely on these, but we have a bunch of old and
   175→	// sloppy tests that don't always populate arguments properly.
   176→	if config == nil {
   177→		config = configs.NewEmptyConfig()
   178→	}
   179→	if prevRunState == nil {
   180→		prevRunState = states.NewState()
   181→	}
   182→	if opts == nil {
   183→		opts = &PlanOpts{
   184→			Mode: plans.NormalMode,
   185→		}
   186→	}
   187→
   188→	moreDiags := c.checkConfigDependencies(config)
   189→	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   190→	moreDiags = c.checkStateDependencies(prevRunState)
   191→	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   192→
   193→	// If required dependencies are not available then we'll bail early since
   194→	// otherwise we're likely to just see a bunch of other errors related to
   195→	// incompatibilities, which could be overwhelming for the user.
   196→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   197→		return nil, nil, diags
   198→	}
   199→
   200→	providerCfgDiags := checkExternalProviders(config, nil, prevRunState, opts.ExternalProviders)
   201→	diags = diags.Append(providerCfgDiags)
   202→	if providerCfgDiags.HasErrors() {
   203→		return nil, nil, diags
   204→	}
   205→
   206→	switch opts.Mode {
   207→	case plans.NormalMode, plans.DestroyMode:
   208→		// OK
   209→	case plans.RefreshOnlyMode:
   210→		if opts.SkipRefresh {
   211→			// The CLI layer (and other similar callers) should prevent this
   212→			// combination of options.
   213→			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   214→				tfdiags.Error,
   215→				"Incompatible plan options",
   216→				"Cannot skip refreshing in refresh-only mode. This is a bug in Terraform.",
   217→			))
   218→			return nil, nil, diags
   219→		}
   220→	default:
   221→		// The CLI layer (and other similar callers) should not try to
   222→		// create a context for a mode that Terraform Core doesn't support.
   223→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   224→			tfdiags.Error,
   225→			"Unsupported plan mode",
   226→			fmt.Sprintf("Terraform Core doesn't know how to handle plan mode %s. This is a bug in Terraform.", opts.Mode),
   227→		))
   228→		return nil, nil, diags
   229→	}
   230→	if len(opts.ForceReplace) > 0 && opts.Mode != plans.NormalMode {
   231→		// The other modes don't generate no-op or update actions that we might
   232→		// upgrade to be "replace", so doesn't make sense to combine those.
   233→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   234→			tfdiags.Error,
   235→			"Unsupported plan mode",
   236→			"Forcing resource instance replacement (with -replace=...) is allowed only in normal planning mode.",
   237→		))
   238→		return nil, nil, diags
   239→	}
   240→	if opts.Forget && opts.Mode != plans.DestroyMode {
   241→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   242→			tfdiags.Error,
   243→			"Unsupported plan mode",
   244→			"Forgetting all resources is only allowed in the context of a destroy plan. This is a bug in Terraform, please report it.",
   245→		))
   246→		return nil, nil, diags
   247→	}
   248→
   249→	// By the time we get here, we should have values defined for all of
   250→	// the root module variables, even if some of them are "unknown". It's the
   251→	// caller's responsibility to have already handled the decoding of these
   252→	// from the various ways the CLI allows them to be set and to produce
   253→	// user-friendly error messages if they are not all present, and so
   254→	// the error message from checkInputVariables should never be seen and
   255→	// includes language asking the user to report a bug.
   256→	varDiags := checkInputVariables(config.Module.Variables, opts.SetVariables)
   257→	diags = diags.Append(varDiags)
   258→
   259→	if len(opts.Targets) > 0 {
   260→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   261→			tfdiags.Warning,
   262→			"Resource targeting is in effect",
   263→			`You are creating a plan with the -target option, which means that the result of this plan may not represent all of the changes requested by the current configuration.
   264→
   265→The -target option is not for routine use, and is provided only for exceptional situations such as recovering from errors or mistakes, or when Terraform specifically suggests to use it as part of an error message.`,
   266→		))
   267→	}
   268→
   269→	var plan *plans.Plan
   270→	var planDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   271→	var evalScope *lang.Scope
   272→	switch opts.Mode {
   273→	case plans.NormalMode:
   274→		plan, evalScope, planDiags = c.plan(config, prevRunState, opts)
   275→	case plans.DestroyMode:
   276→		plan, evalScope, planDiags = c.destroyPlan(config, prevRunState, opts)
   277→	case plans.RefreshOnlyMode:
   278→		plan, evalScope, planDiags = c.refreshOnlyPlan(config, prevRunState, opts)
   279→	default:
   280→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("unsupported plan mode %s", opts.Mode))
   281→	}
   282→	diags = diags.Append(planDiags)
   283→	// NOTE: We're intentionally not returning early when diags.HasErrors
   284→	// here because we'll still populate other metadata below on a best-effort
   285→	// basis to try to give the UI some extra context to return alongside the
   286→	// error messages.
   287→
   288→	// convert the variables into the format expected for the plan
   289→	varVals := make(map[string]plans.DynamicValue, len(opts.SetVariables))
   290→	varMarks := make(map[string][]cty.PathValueMarks, len(opts.SetVariables))
   291→	applyTimeVariables := collections.NewSetCmp[string]()
   292→	for k, iv := range opts.SetVariables {
   293→		// If any input variables were declared as ephemeral and set to a
   294→		// non-null value then those variables must be provided again (possibly
   295→		// with _different_ non-null values) during the apply phase.
   296→		if vc, ok := config.Module.Variables[k]; ok && vc.Ephemeral {
   297→			// FIXME: We should actually do this based on the final value
   298→			// in the named values state, rather than the value as provided
   299→			// by the caller, so we can take into account the transforms
   300→			// done during variable evaluation. This is a plausible starting
   301→			// point for now, though.
   302→			if iv.Value != cty.NilVal && !iv.Value.IsNull() {
   303→				applyTimeVariables.Add(k)
   304→			}
   305→			continue
   306→		}
   307→
   308→		// Non-ephemeral variables must remain unchanged between plan and
   309→		// apply, so we'll record their actual values.
   310→		if iv.Value == cty.NilVal {
   311→			continue // We only record values that the caller actually set
   312→		}
   313→
   314→		// Root variable values arriving from the traditional CLI path are
   315→		// unmarked, as they are directly decoded from .tfvars, CLI arguments,
   316→		// or the environment. However, variable values arriving from other
   317→		// plans (via the coordination efforts of the stacks runtime) may have
   318→		// gathered marks during evaluation. We must separate the value from
   319→		// its marks here to maintain compatibility with plans.DynamicValue,
   320→		// which cannot represent marks.
   321→		value, pvm := iv.Value.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()
   322→
   323→		// We use cty.DynamicPseudoType here so that we'll save both the
   324→		// value _and_ its dynamic type in the plan, so we can recover
   325→		// exactly the same value later.
   326→		dv, err := plans.NewDynamicValue(value, cty.DynamicPseudoType)
   327→		if err != nil {
   328→			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   329→				tfdiags.Error,
   330→				"Failed to prepare variable value for plan",
   331→				fmt.Sprintf("The value for variable %q could not be serialized to store in the plan: %s.", k, err),
   332→			))
   333→			continue
   334→		}
   335→		varVals[k] = dv
   336→		varMarks[k] = pvm
   337→	}
   338→
   339→	// insert the run-specific data from the context into the plan; variables,
   340→	// targets and provider SHAs.
   341→	if plan != nil {
   342→		plan.VariableValues = varVals
   343→		plan.ApplyTimeVariables = applyTimeVariables
   344→		if len(varMarks) > 0 {
   345→			plan.VariableMarks = varMarks
   346→		}
   347→		plan.TargetAddrs = opts.Targets
   348→	} else if !diags.HasErrors() {
   349→		panic("nil plan but no errors")
   350→	}
   351→
   352→	if plan != nil {
   353→		relevantAttrs, rDiags := c.relevantResourceAttrsForPlan(config, plan)
   354→		diags = diags.Append(rDiags)
   355→		plan.RelevantAttributes = relevantAttrs
   356→	}
   357→
   358→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   359→		// We can't proceed further with an invalid plan, because an invalid
   360→		// plan isn't applyable by definition.
   361→		if plan != nil {
   362→			// We'll explicitly mark our plan as errored so that it can't
   363→			// be accidentally applied even though it's incomplete.
   364→			plan.Errored = true
   365→		}
   366→		return plan, evalScope, diags
   367→	}
   368→
   369→	diags = diags.Append(c.checkApplyGraph(plan, config, opts))
   370→
   371→	return plan, evalScope, diags
   372→}
   373→
   374→// checkApplyGraph builds the apply graph out of the current plan to
   375→// check for any errors that may arise once the planned changes are added to
   376→// the graph. This allows terraform to report errors (mostly cycles) during
   377→// plan that would otherwise only crop up during apply
   378→func (c *Context) checkApplyGraph(plan *plans.Plan, config *configs.Config, opts *PlanOpts) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   379→	if plan.Changes.Empty() {
   380→		log.Println("[DEBUG] no planned changes, skipping apply graph check")
   381→		return nil
   382→	}
   383→	log.Println("[DEBUG] building apply graph to check for errors")
   384→
   385→	_, _, diags := c.applyGraph(plan, config, opts.ApplyOpts(), true)
   386→	return diags
   387→}
   388→
   389→var DefaultPlanOpts = &PlanOpts{
   390→	Mode: plans.NormalMode,
   391→}
   392→
   393→// SimplePlanOpts is a constructor to help with creating "simple" values of
   394→// PlanOpts which only specify a mode and input variables.
   395→//
   396→// This helper function is primarily intended for use in straightforward
   397→// tests that don't need any of the more "esoteric" planning options. For
   398→// handling real user requests to run Terraform, it'd probably be better
   399→// to construct a *PlanOpts value directly and provide a way for the user
   400→// to set values for all of its fields.
   401→//
   402→// The "mode" and "setVariables" arguments become the values of the "Mode"
   403→// and "SetVariables" fields in the result. Refer to the PlanOpts type
   404→// documentation to learn about the meanings of those fields.
   405→func SimplePlanOpts(mode plans.Mode, setVariables InputValues) *PlanOpts {
   406→	return &PlanOpts{
   407→		Mode:         mode,
   408→		SetVariables: setVariables,
   409→	}
   410→}
   411→
   412→func (c *Context) plan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   413→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   414→	if opts.Mode != plans.NormalMode {
   415→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("called Context.plan with %s", opts.Mode))
   416→	}
   417→
   418→	plan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, prevRunState, opts)
   419→	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   420→
   421→	return plan, evalScope, diags
   422→}
   423→
   424→func (c *Context) refreshOnlyPlan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   425→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   426→
   427→	if opts.Mode != plans.RefreshOnlyMode {
   428→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("called Context.refreshOnlyPlan with %s", opts.Mode))
   429→	}
   430→
   431→	plan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, prevRunState, opts)
   432→	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   433→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   434→		// Non-nil plan along with errors indicates a non-applyable partial
   435→		// plan that's only suitable to be shown to the user as extra context
   436→		// to help understand the errors.
   437→		return plan, evalScope, diags
   438→	}
   439→
   440→	// If the graph builder and graph nodes correctly obeyed our directive
   441→	// to refresh only, the set of resource changes should always be empty.
   442→	// We'll safety-check that here so we can return a clear message about it,
   443→	// rather than probably just generating confusing output at the UI layer.
   444→	if len(plan.Changes.Resources) != 0 {
   445→		// Some extra context in the logs in case the user reports this message
   446→		// as a bug, as a starting point for debugging.
   447→		for _, rc := range plan.Changes.Resources {
   448→			if depKey := rc.DeposedKey; depKey == states.NotDeposed {
   449→				log.Printf("[DEBUG] Refresh-only plan includes %s change for %s", rc.Action, rc.Addr)
   450→			} else {
   451→				log.Printf("[DEBUG] Refresh-only plan includes %s change for %s deposed object %s", rc.Action, rc.Addr, depKey)
   452→			}
   453→		}
   454→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   455→			tfdiags.Error,
   456→			"Invalid refresh-only plan",
   457→			"Terraform generated planned resource changes in a refresh-only plan. This is a bug in Terraform.",
   458→		))
   459→	}
   460→
   461→	// We don't populate RelevantResources for a refresh-only plan, because
   462→	// they never have any planned actions and so no resource can ever be
   463→	// "relevant" per the intended meaning of that field.
   464→
   465→	return plan, evalScope, diags
   466→}
   467→
   468→func (c *Context) destroyPlan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   469→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   470→
   471→	if opts.Mode != plans.DestroyMode {
   472→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("called Context.destroyPlan with %s", opts.Mode))
   473→	}
   474→
   475→	priorState := prevRunState
   476→
   477→	// A destroy plan starts by running Refresh to read any pending data
   478→	// sources, and remove missing managed resources. This is required because
   479→	// a "destroy plan" is only creating delete changes, and is essentially a
   480→	// local operation.
   481→	//
   482→	// NOTE: if skipRefresh _is_ set then we'll rely on the destroy-plan walk
   483→	// below to upgrade the prevRunState and priorState both to the latest
   484→	// resource type schemas, so NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance.Execute
   485→	// must coordinate with this by taking that action only when c.skipRefresh
   486→	// _is_ set. This coupling between the two is unfortunate but necessary
   487→	// to work within our current structure.
   488→	if !opts.SkipRefresh && !prevRunState.Empty() {
   489→		log.Printf("[TRACE] Context.destroyPlan: calling Context.plan to get the effect of refreshing the prior state")
   490→		refreshOpts := *opts
   491→		refreshOpts.Mode = plans.NormalMode
   492→		refreshOpts.PreDestroyRefresh = true
   493→
   494→		// FIXME: A normal plan is required here to refresh the state, because
   495→		// the state and configuration may not match during a destroy, and a
   496→		// normal refresh plan can fail with evaluation errors. In the future
   497→		// the destroy plan should take care of refreshing instances itself,
   498→		// where the special cases of evaluation and skipping condition checks
   499→		// can be done.
   500→		refreshPlan, _, refreshDiags := c.plan(config, prevRunState, &refreshOpts)
   501→		if refreshDiags.HasErrors() {
   502→			// NOTE: Normally we'd append diagnostics regardless of whether
   503→			// there are errors, just in case there are warnings we'd want to
   504→			// preserve, but we're intentionally _not_ doing that here because
   505→			// if the first plan succeeded then we'll be running another plan
   506→			// in DestroyMode below, and we don't want to double-up any
   507→			// warnings that both plan walks would generate.
   508→			// (This does mean we won't show any warnings that would've been
   509→			// unique to only this walk, but we're assuming here that if the
   510→			// warnings aren't also applicable to a destroy plan then we'd
   511→			// rather not show them here, because this non-destroy plan for
   512→			// refreshing is largely an implementation detail.)
   513→			diags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)
   514→			return nil, nil, diags
   515→		}
   516→
   517→		// We'll use the refreshed state -- which is the  "prior state" from
   518→		// the perspective of this "destroy plan" -- as the starting state
   519→		// for our destroy-plan walk, so it can take into account if we
   520→		// detected during refreshing that anything was already deleted outside
   521→		// of Terraform.
   522→		priorState = refreshPlan.PriorState.DeepCopy()
   523→
   524→		// The refresh plan may have upgraded state for some resources, make
   525→		// sure we store the new version.
   526→		prevRunState = refreshPlan.PrevRunState.DeepCopy()
   527→		log.Printf("[TRACE] Context.destroyPlan: now _really_ creating a destroy plan")
   528→	}
   529→
   530→	destroyPlan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, priorState, opts)
   531→	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   532→	if walkDiags.HasErrors() {
   533→		// Non-nil plan along with errors indicates a non-applyable partial
   534→		// plan that's only suitable to be shown to the user as extra context
   535→		// to help understand the errors.
   536→		return destroyPlan, evalScope, diags
   537→	}
   538→
   539→	if !opts.SkipRefresh {
   540→		// If we didn't skip refreshing then we want the previous run state to
   541→		// be the one we originally fed into the c.refreshOnlyPlan call above,
   542→		// not the refreshed version we used for the destroy planWalk.
   543→		destroyPlan.PrevRunState = prevRunState
   544→	}
   545→
   546→	relevantAttrs, rDiags := c.relevantResourceAttrsForPlan(config, destroyPlan)
   547→	diags = diags.Append(rDiags)
   548→
   549→	destroyPlan.RelevantAttributes = relevantAttrs
   550→	return destroyPlan, evalScope, diags
   551→}
   552→
   553→func (c *Context) prePlanFindAndApplyMoves(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State) ([]refactoring.MoveStatement, refactoring.MoveResults, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   554→	explicitMoveStmts := refactoring.FindMoveStatements(config)
   555→	implicitMoveStmts := refactoring.ImpliedMoveStatements(config, prevRunState, explicitMoveStmts)
   556→	var moveStmts []refactoring.MoveStatement
   557→	if stmtsLen := len(explicitMoveStmts) + len(implicitMoveStmts); stmtsLen > 0 {
   558→		moveStmts = make([]refactoring.MoveStatement, 0, stmtsLen)
   559→		moveStmts = append(moveStmts, explicitMoveStmts...)
   560→		moveStmts = append(moveStmts, implicitMoveStmts...)
   561→	}
   562→	moveResults, diags := refactoring.ApplyMoves(moveStmts, prevRunState, c.plugins.ProviderFactories())
   563→	return moveStmts, moveResults, diags
   564→}
   565→
   566→func (c *Context) prePlanVerifyTargetedMoves(moveResults refactoring.MoveResults, targets []addrs.Targetable) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   567→	if len(targets) < 1 {
   568→		return nil // the following only matters when targeting
   569→	}
   570→
   571→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   572→
   573→	var excluded []addrs.AbsResourceInstance
   574→	for _, result := range moveResults.Changes.Values() {
   575→		fromMatchesTarget := false
   576→		toMatchesTarget := false
   577→		for _, targetAddr := range targets {
   578→			if targetAddr.TargetContains(result.From) {
   579→				fromMatchesTarget = true
   580→			}
   581→			if targetAddr.TargetContains(result.To) {
   582→				toMatchesTarget = true
   583→			}
   584→		}
   585→		if !fromMatchesTarget {
   586→			excluded = append(excluded, result.From)
   587→		}
   588→		if !toMatchesTarget {
   589→			excluded = append(excluded, result.To)
   590→		}
   591→	}
   592→	if len(excluded) > 0 {
   593→		sort.Slice(excluded, func(i, j int) bool {
   594→			return excluded[i].Less(excluded[j])
   595→		})
   596→
   597→		var listBuf strings.Builder
   598→		var prevResourceAddr addrs.AbsResource
   599→		for _, instAddr := range excluded {
   600→			// Targeting generally ends up selecting whole resources rather
   601→			// than individual instances, because we don't factor in
   602→			// individual instances until DynamicExpand, so we're going to
   603→			// always show whole resource addresses here, excluding any
   604→			// instance keys. (This also neatly avoids dealing with the
   605→			// different quoting styles required for string instance keys
   606→			// on different shells, which is handy.)
   607→			//
   608→			// To avoid showing duplicates when we have multiple instances
   609→			// of the same resource, we'll remember the most recent
   610→			// resource we rendered in prevResource, which is sufficient
   611→			// because we sorted the list of instance addresses above, and
   612→			// our sort order always groups together instances of the same
   613→			// resource.
   614→			resourceAddr := instAddr.ContainingResource()
   615→			if resourceAddr.Equal(prevResourceAddr) {
   616→				continue
   617→			}
   618→			fmt.Fprintf(&listBuf, "\n  -target=%q", resourceAddr.String())
   619→			prevResourceAddr = resourceAddr
   620→		}
   621→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   622→			tfdiags.Error,
   623→			"Moved resource instances excluded by targeting",
   624→			fmt.Sprintf(
   625→				"Resource instances in your current state have moved to new addresses in the latest configuration. Terraform must include those resource instances while planning in order to ensure a correct result, but your -target=... options do not fully cover all of those resource instances.\n\nTo create a valid plan, either remove your -target=... options altogether or add the following additional target options:%s\n\nNote that adding these options may include further additional resource instances in your plan, in order to respect object dependencies.",
   626→				listBuf.String(),
   627→			),
   628→		))
   629→	}
   630→
   631→	return diags
   632→}
   633→
   634→func (c *Context) postPlanValidateMoves(config *configs.Config, stmts []refactoring.MoveStatement, allInsts instances.Set) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   635→	return refactoring.ValidateMoves(stmts, config, allInsts)
   636→}
   637→
   638→// findImportTargets builds a list of import targets from any import blocks in
   639→// config.
   640→func (c *Context) findImportTargets(config *configs.Config) []*ImportTarget {
   641→	var importTargets []*ImportTarget
   642→	for _, ic := range config.Module.Import {
   643→		importTargets = append(importTargets, &ImportTarget{
   644→			Config: ic,
   645→		})
   646→	}
   647→	return importTargets
   648→}
   649→
   650→// findForgetTargets builds a list of resources and a list of modules to be
   651→// forgotten, based on any removed blocks in config.
   652→func (c *Context) findForgetTargets(config *configs.Config) (forgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource, forgetModules []addrs.Module, diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   653→	removeStmts, diags := refactoring.FindRemoveStatements(config)
   654→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   655→		return nil, nil, diags
   656→	}
   657→	for _, rst := range removeStmts.Values() {
   658→		if rst.Destroy {
   659→			// no-op
   660→		} else {
   661→			if fr, ok := rst.From.(addrs.ConfigResource); ok {
   662→				forgetResources = append(forgetResources, fr)
   663→			} else if fm, ok := rst.From.(addrs.Module); ok {
   664→				forgetModules = append(forgetModules, fm)
   665→			} else {
   666→				panic("Invalid ConfigMoveable type in remove statement")
   667→			}
   668→		}
   669→	}
   670→	return forgetResources, forgetModules, diags
   671→}
   672→
   673→func (c *Context) planWalk(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   674→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   675→	log.Printf("[DEBUG] Building and walking plan graph for %s", opts.Mode)
   676→
   677→	prevRunState = prevRunState.DeepCopy() // don't modify the caller's object when we process the moves
   678→	moveStmts, moveResults, moveDiags := c.prePlanFindAndApplyMoves(config, prevRunState)
   679→	diags = diags.Append(moveDiags)
   680→	if moveDiags.HasErrors() {
   681→		return nil, nil, diags
   682→	}
   683→
   684→	// If resource targeting is in effect then it might conflict with the
   685→	// move result.
   686→	diags = diags.Append(c.prePlanVerifyTargetedMoves(moveResults, opts.Targets))
   687→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   688→		// We'll return early here, because if we have any moved resource
   689→		// instances excluded by targeting then planning is likely to encounter
   690→		// strange problems that may lead to confusing error messages.
   691→		return nil, nil, diags
   692→	}
   693→
   694→	graph, walkOp, moreDiags := c.planGraph(config, prevRunState, opts)
   695→	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   696→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   697→		return nil, nil, diags
   698→	}
   699→
   700→	timestamp := time.Now().UTC()
   701→	if opts.ForcePlanTimestamp != nil {
   702→		// Some tests use this to produce stable results to assert against.
   703→		timestamp = *opts.ForcePlanTimestamp
   704→	}
   705→
   706→	var externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
   707→	if opts != nil {
   708→		externalProviderConfigs = opts.ExternalProviders
   709→	}
   710→
   711→	// If we get here then we should definitely have a non-nil "graph", which
   712→	// we can now walk.
   713→	changes := plans.NewChanges()
   714→
   715→	// Initialize the results table to validate provider function calls.
   716→	// Hold reference to this so we can store the table data in the plan file.
   717→	providerFuncResults := providers.NewFunctionResultsTable(nil)
   718→
   719→	walker, walkDiags := c.walk(graph, walkOp, &graphWalkOpts{
   720→		Config:                     config,
   721→		InputState:                 prevRunState,
   722→		ExternalProviderConfigs:    externalProviderConfigs,
   723→		DeferralAllowed:            opts.DeferralAllowed,
   724→		ExternalDependencyDeferred: opts.ExternalDependencyDeferred,
   725→		Changes:                    changes,
   726→		MoveResults:                moveResults,
   727→		Overrides:                  opts.Overrides,
   728→		PlanTimeTimestamp:          timestamp,
   729→		ProviderFuncResults:        providerFuncResults,
   730→		Forget:                     opts.Forget,
   731→	})
   732→	diags = diags.Append(walker.NonFatalDiagnostics)
   733→	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   734→
   735→	allInsts := walker.InstanceExpander.AllInstances()
   736→
   737→	moveValidateDiags := c.postPlanValidateMoves(config, moveStmts, allInsts)
   738→	if moveValidateDiags.HasErrors() {
   739→		// If any of the move statements are invalid then those errors take
   740→		// precedence over any other errors because an incomplete move graph
   741→		// is quite likely to be the _cause_ of various errors. This oddity
   742→		// comes from the fact that we need to apply the moves before we
   743→		// actually validate them, because validation depends on the result
   744→		// of first trying to plan.
   745→		return nil, nil, moveValidateDiags
   746→	}
   747→	diags = diags.Append(moveValidateDiags) // might just contain warnings
   748→
   749→	if moveResults.Blocked.Len() > 0 && !diags.HasErrors() {
   750→		// If we had blocked moves and we're not going to be returning errors
   751→		// then we'll report the blockers as a warning. We do this only in the
   752→		// absense of errors because invalid move statements might well be
   753→		// the root cause of the blockers, and so better to give an actionable
   754→		// error message than a less-actionable warning.
   755→		diags = diags.Append(blockedMovesWarningDiag(moveResults))
   756→	}
   757→
   758→	// If we reach this point with error diagnostics then "changes" is a
   759→	// representation of the subset of changes we were able to plan before
   760→	// we encountered errors, which we'll return as part of a non-nil plan
   761→	// so that e.g. the UI can show what was planned so far in case that extra
   762→	// context helps the user to understand the error messages we're returning.
   763→	prevRunState = walker.PrevRunState.Close()
   764→
   765→	// The refreshed state may have data resource objects which were deferred
   766→	// to apply and cannot be serialized.
   767→	walker.RefreshState.RemovePlannedResourceInstanceObjects()
   768→	priorState := walker.RefreshState.Close()
   769→
   770→	driftedResources, driftDiags := c.driftedResources(config, prevRunState, priorState, moveResults)
   771→	diags = diags.Append(driftDiags)
   772→
   773→	deferredResources, deferredDiags := c.deferredResources(config, walker.Deferrals.GetDeferredChanges(), priorState)
   774→	diags = diags.Append(deferredDiags)
   775→
   776→	var forgottenResources []string
   777→	for _, rc := range changes.Resources {
   778→		if rc.Action == plans.Forget {
   779→			// TODO KEM display resource ids
   780→			forgottenResources = append(forgottenResources, fmt.Sprintf(" - %s", rc.Addr))
   781→		}
   782→	}
   783→	if len(forgottenResources) > 0 {
   784→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   785→			tfdiags.Warning,
   786→			"Some objects will no longer be managed by Terraform",
   787→			fmt.Sprintf("If you apply this plan, Terraform will discard its tracking information for the following objects, but it will not delete them:\n%s\n\nAfter applying this plan, Terraform will no longer manage these objects. You will need to import them into Terraform to manage them again.", strings.Join(forgottenResources, "\n")),
   788→		))
   789→	}
   790→	schemas, schemaDiags := c.Schemas(config, prevRunState)
   791→	// We must finish building a plan object, and cannot return early here.
   792→	var changesSrc *plans.ChangesSrc
   793→	var err error
   794→	if !schemaDiags.HasErrors() {
   795→		changesSrc, err = changes.Encode(schemas)
   796→		if err != nil {
   797→			diags = diags.Append(err)
   798→		}
   799→	}
   800→
   801→	plan := &plans.Plan{
   802→		UIMode:                  opts.Mode,
   803→		Changes:                 changesSrc,
   804→		DriftedResources:        driftedResources,
   805→		DeferredResources:       deferredResources,
   806→		PrevRunState:            prevRunState,
   807→		PriorState:              priorState,
   808→		ExternalReferences:      opts.ExternalReferences,
   809→		Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   810→		Checks:                  states.NewCheckResults(walker.Checks),
   811→		Timestamp:               timestamp,
   812→		ProviderFunctionResults: providerFuncResults.GetHashes(),
   813→
   814→		// Other fields get populated by Context.Plan after we return
   815→	}
   816→
   817→	// Our final rulings on whether the plan is "complete" and "applyable".
   818→	// See the documentation for these plan fields to learn what exactly they
   819→	// are intended to mean.
   820→	if !diags.HasErrors() {
   821→		if len(opts.Targets) == 0 && !walker.Deferrals.HaveAnyDeferrals() {
   822→			// A plan without any targets or deferred actions should be
   823→			// complete if we didn't encounter errors while producing it.
   824→			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is complete")
   825→			plan.Complete = true
   826→		} else {
   827→			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is incomplete")
   828→		}
   829→		if opts.Mode == plans.RefreshOnlyMode {
   830→			// In refresh-only mode we explicitly don't expect to propose any
   831→			// actions, but the plan is applyable if the state was changed
   832→			// in an interesting way by the refresh step.
   833→			plan.Applyable = !plan.PriorState.ManagedResourcesEqual(plan.PrevRunState) || !plan.PriorState.RootOutputValuesEqual(plan.PrevRunState)
   834→		} else {
   835→			// For other planning modes a plan is applyable if its "changes"
   836→			// are not considered empty (by whatever rules the plans package
   837→			// uses to decide that).
   838→			plan.Applyable = !plan.Changes.Empty()
   839→		}
   840→		if plan.Applyable {
   841→			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is applyable")
   842→		} else {
   843→			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is not applyable")
   844→		}
   845→	} else {
   846→		log.Println("[WARN] Planning encountered errors, so plan is not applyable")
   847→	}
   848→
   849→	// The caller also gets access to an expression evaluation scope in the
   850→	// root module, in case it needs to extract other information using
   851→	// expressions, like in "terraform console" or the test harness.
   852→	evalScope := evalScopeFromGraphWalk(walker, addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   853→
   854→	return plan, evalScope, diags
   855→}
   856→
   857→func (c *Context) deferredResources(config *configs.Config, deferrals []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChange, state *states.State) ([]*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   858→	var deferredResources []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc
   859→
   860→	schemas, diags := c.Schemas(config, state)
   861→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   862→		return deferredResources, diags
   863→	}
   864→
   865→	for _, deferral := range deferrals {
   866→
   867→		schema, _ := schemas.ResourceTypeConfig(
   868→			deferral.Change.ProviderAddr.Provider,
   869→			deferral.Change.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode,
   870→			deferral.Change.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type)
   871→
   872→		ty := schema.ImpliedType()
   873→		deferralSrc, err := deferral.Encode(ty)
   874→		if err != nil {
   875→			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   876→				tfdiags.Error,
   877→				"Failed to prepare deferred resource for plan",
   878→				fmt.Sprintf("The deferred resource %q could not be serialized to store in the plan: %s.", deferral.Change.Addr, err)))
   879→			continue
   880→		}
   881→
   882→		deferredResources = append(deferredResources, deferralSrc)
   883→	}
   884→	return deferredResources, diags
   885→}
   886→
   887→func (c *Context) planGraph(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*Graph, walkOperation, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   888→	var externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
   889→	if opts != nil {
   890→		externalProviderConfigs = opts.ExternalProviders
   891→	}
   892→
   893→	switch mode := opts.Mode; mode {
   894→	case plans.NormalMode:
   895→		// In Normal mode we need to pay attention to import and removed blocks
   896→		// in config so their targets can be added to the graph.
   897→		forgetResources, forgetModules, diags := c.findForgetTargets(config)
   898→		if diags.HasErrors() {
   899→			return nil, walkPlan, diags
   900→		}
   901→		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
   902→			Config:                  config,
   903→			State:                   prevRunState,
   904→			RootVariableValues:      opts.SetVariables,
   905→			ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
   906→			Plugins:                 c.plugins,
   907→			Targets:                 opts.Targets,
   908→			ForceReplace:            opts.ForceReplace,
   909→			skipRefresh:             opts.SkipRefresh,
   910→			preDestroyRefresh:       opts.PreDestroyRefresh,
   911→			Operation:               walkPlan,
   912→			ExternalReferences:      opts.ExternalReferences,
   913→			Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   914→			ImportTargets:           c.findImportTargets(config),
   915→			forgetResources:         forgetResources,
   916→			forgetModules:           forgetModules,
   917→			GenerateConfigPath:      opts.GenerateConfigPath,
   918→		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   919→		return graph, walkPlan, diags
   920→	case plans.RefreshOnlyMode:
   921→		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
   922→			Config:                  config,
   923→			State:                   prevRunState,
   924→			RootVariableValues:      opts.SetVariables,
   925→			ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
   926→			Plugins:                 c.plugins,
   927→			Targets:                 opts.Targets,
   928→			skipRefresh:             opts.SkipRefresh,
   929→			skipPlanChanges:         true, // this activates "refresh only" mode.
   930→			Operation:               walkPlan,
   931→			ExternalReferences:      opts.ExternalReferences,
   932→			Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   933→		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   934→		return graph, walkPlan, diags
   935→	case plans.DestroyMode:
   936→		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
   937→			Config:                  config,
   938→			State:                   prevRunState,
   939→			RootVariableValues:      opts.SetVariables,
   940→			ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
   941→			Plugins:                 c.plugins,
   942→			Targets:                 opts.Targets,
   943→			skipRefresh:             opts.SkipRefresh,
   944→			Operation:               walkPlanDestroy,
   945→			Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   946→		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   947→		return graph, walkPlanDestroy, diags
   948→	default:
   949→		// The above should cover all plans.Mode values
   950→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("unsupported plan mode %s", mode))
   951→	}
   952→}
   953→
   954→// driftedResources is a best-effort attempt to compare the current and prior
   955→// state. If we cannot decode the prior state for some reason, this should only
   956→// return warnings to help the user correlate any missing resources in the
   957→// report. This is known to happen when targeting a subset of resources,
   958→// because the excluded instances will have been removed from the plan and
   959→// not upgraded.
   960→func (c *Context) driftedResources(config *configs.Config, oldState, newState *states.State, moves refactoring.MoveResults) ([]*plans.ResourceInstanceChangeSrc, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   961→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   962→
   963→	if newState.ManagedResourcesEqual(oldState) && moves.Changes.Len() == 0 {
   964→		// Nothing to do, because we only detect and report drift for managed
   965→		// resource instances.
   966→		return nil, diags
   967→	}
   968→
   969→	schemas, schemaDiags := c.Schemas(config, newState)
   970→	diags = diags.Append(schemaDiags)
   971→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   972→		return nil, diags
   973→	}
   974→
   975→	var drs []*plans.ResourceInstanceChangeSrc
   976→
   977→	for _, ms := range oldState.Modules {
   978→		for _, rs := range ms.Resources {
   979→			if rs.Addr.Resource.Mode != addrs.ManagedResourceMode {
   980→				// Drift reporting is only for managed resources
   981→				continue
   982→			}
   983→
   984→			provider := rs.ProviderConfig.Provider
   985→			for key, oldIS := range rs.Instances {
   986→				if oldIS.Current == nil {
   987→					// Not interested in instances that only have deposed objects
   988→					continue
   989→				}
   990→				addr := rs.Addr.Instance(key)
   991→
   992→				// Previous run address defaults to the current address, but
   993→				// can differ if the resource moved before refreshing
   994→				prevRunAddr := addr
   995→				if move, ok := moves.Changes.GetOk(addr); ok {
   996→					prevRunAddr = move.From
   997→				}
   998→
   999→				newIS := newState.ResourceInstance(addr)
  1000→
  1001→				schema, _ := schemas.ResourceTypeConfig(
  1002→					provider,
  1003→					addr.Resource.Resource.Mode,
  1004→					addr.Resource.Resource.Type,
  1005→				)
  1006→				if schema == nil {
  1007→					diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
  1008→						tfdiags.Warning,
  1009→						"Missing resource schema from provider",
  1010→						fmt.Sprintf("No resource schema found for %s when decoding prior state", addr.Resource.Resource.Type),
  1011→					))
  1012→					continue
  1013→				}
  1014→				ty := schema.ImpliedType()
  1015→
  1016→				oldObj, err := oldIS.Current.Decode(ty)
  1017→				if err != nil {
  1018→					diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
  1019→						tfdiags.Warning,
  1020→						"Failed to decode resource from state",
  1021→						fmt.Sprintf("Error decoding %q from prior state: %s", addr.String(), err),
  1022→					))
  1023→					continue
  1024→				}
  1025→
  1026→				var newObj *states.ResourceInstanceObject
  1027→				if newIS != nil && newIS.Current != nil {
  1028→					newObj, err = newIS.Current.Decode(ty)
  1029→					if err != nil {
  1030→						diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
  1031→							tfdiags.Warning,
  1032→							"Failed to decode resource from state",
  1033→							fmt.Sprintf("Error decoding %q from prior state: %s", addr.String(), err),
  1034→						))
  1035→						continue
  1036→					}
  1037→				}
  1038→
  1039→				var oldVal, newVal cty.Value
  1040→				oldVal = oldObj.Value
  1041→				if newObj != nil {
  1042→					newVal = newObj.Value
  1043→				} else {
  1044→					newVal = cty.NullVal(ty)
  1045→				}
  1046→
  1047→				if oldVal.RawEquals(newVal) && addr.Equal(prevRunAddr) {
  1048→					// No drift if the two values are semantically equivalent
  1049→					// and no move has happened
  1050→					continue
  1051→				}
  1052→
  1053→				// We can detect three types of changes after refreshing state,
  1054→				// only two of which are easily understood as "drift":
  1055→				//
  1056→				// - Resources which were deleted outside of Terraform;
  1057→				// - Resources where the object value has changed outside of
  1058→				//   Terraform;
  1059→				// - Resources which have been moved without other changes.
  1060→				//
  1061→				// All of these are returned as drift, to allow refresh-only plans
  1062→				// to present a full set of changes which will be applied.
  1063→				var action plans.Action
  1064→				switch {
  1065→				case newVal.IsNull():
  1066→					action = plans.Delete
  1067→				case !oldVal.RawEquals(newVal):
  1068→					action = plans.Update
  1069→				default:
  1070→					action = plans.NoOp
  1071→				}
  1072→
  1073→				change := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{
  1074→					Addr:         addr,
  1075→					PrevRunAddr:  prevRunAddr,
  1076→					ProviderAddr: rs.ProviderConfig,
  1077→					Change: plans.Change{
  1078→						Action: action,
  1079→						Before: oldVal,
  1080→						After:  newVal,
  1081→					},
  1082→				}
  1083→
  1084→				changeSrc, err := change.Encode(ty)
  1085→				if err != nil {
  1086→					diags = diags.Append(err)
  1087→					return nil, diags
  1088→				}
  1089→
  1090→				drs = append(drs, changeSrc)
  1091→			}
  1092→		}
  1093→	}
  1094→
  1095→	return drs, diags
  1096→}
  1097→
  1098→// PlanGraphForUI is a last vestage of graphs in the public interface of Context
  1099→// (as opposed to graphs as an implementation detail) intended only for use
  1100→// by the "terraform graph" command when asked to render a plan-time graph.
  1101→//
  1102→// The result of this is intended only for rendering to the user as a dot
  1103→// graph, and so may change in future in order to make the result more useful
  1104→// in that context, even if drifts away from the physical graph that Terraform
  1105→// Core currently uses as an implementation detail of planning.
  1106→func (c *Context) PlanGraphForUI(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, mode plans.Mode) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
  1107→	// For now though, this really is just the internal graph, confusing
  1108→	// implementation details and all.
  1109→
  1110→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
  1111→
  1112→	opts := &PlanOpts{Mode: mode}
  1113→
  1114→	graph, _, moreDiags := c.planGraph(config, prevRunState, opts)
  1115→	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
  1116→	return graph, diags
  1117→}
  1118→
  1119→func blockedMovesWarningDiag(results refactoring.MoveResults) tfdiags.Diagnostic {
  1120→	if results.Blocked.Len() < 1 {
  1121→		// Caller should check first
  1122→		panic("request to render blocked moves warning without any blocked moves")
  1123→	}
  1124→
  1125→	var itemsBuf bytes.Buffer
  1126→	for _, blocked := range results.Blocked.Values() {
  1127→		fmt.Fprintf(&itemsBuf, "\n  - %s could not move to %s", blocked.Actual, blocked.Wanted)
  1128→	}
  1129→
  1130→	return tfdiags.Sourceless(
  1131→		tfdiags.Warning,
  1132→		"Unresolved resource instance address changes",
  1133→		fmt.Sprintf(
  1134→			"Terraform tried to adjust resource instance addresses in the prior state based on change information recorded in the configuration, but some adjustments did not succeed due to existing objects already at the intended addresses:%s\n\nTerraform has planned to destroy these objects. If Terraform's proposed changes aren't appropriate, you must first resolve the conflicts using the \"terraform state\" subcommands and then create a new plan.",

  1140→// referenceAnalyzer returns a globalref.Analyzer object to help with
  1141→// global analysis of references within the configuration that's attached
  1142→// to the receiving context.
  1143→func (c *Context) referenceAnalyzer(config *configs.Config, state *states.State) (*globalref.Analyzer, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
  1144→	schemas, diags := c.Schemas(config, state)
  1145→	if diags.HasErrors() {
  1146→		return nil, diags
  1147→	}
  1148→	return globalref.NewAnalyzer(config, schemas.Providers), diags
  1149→}

  1151→// relevantResourcesForPlan implements the heuristic we use to populate the
  1152→// RelevantResources field of returned plans.
  1153→func (c *Context) relevantResourceAttrsForPlan(config *configs.Config, plan *plans.Plan) ([]globalref.ResourceAttr, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
  1154→	azr, diags := c.referenceAnalyzer(config, plan.PriorState)
  1155→	if diags.HasErrors() {
  1156→		return nil, diags
  1157→	}
  1158→
  1159→	var refs []globalref.Reference
  1160→	for _, change := range plan.Changes.Resources {
  1161→		if change.Action == plans.NoOp {
  1162→			continue
  1163→		}
  1164→
  1165→		moreRefs := azr.ReferencesFromResourceInstance(change.Addr)
  1166→		refs = append(refs, moreRefs...)
  1167→	}
  1168→
  1169→	for _, change := range plan.Changes.Outputs {
  1170→		if change.Action == plans.NoOp {
  1171→			continue
  1172→		}
  1173→
  1174→		moreRefs := azr.ReferencesFromOutputValue(change.Addr)
  1175→		refs = append(refs, moreRefs...)
  1176→	}
  1177→
  1178→	var contributors []globalref.ResourceAttr
  1179→
  1180→	for _, ref := range azr.ContributingResourceReferences(refs...) {
  1181→		if res, ok := ref.ResourceAttr(); ok {
  1182→			contributors = append(contributors, res)
  1183→		}
  1184→	}
  1185→
  1186→	return contributors, diags
  1187→}

   140→// Plan generates an execution plan by comparing the given configuration
   141→// with the given previous run state.
   142→//
   143→// The given planning options allow control of various other details of the
   144→// planning process that are not represented directly in the configuration.
   145→// You can use terraform.DefaultPlanOpts to generate a normal plan with no
   146→// special options.
   147→//
   148→// If the returned diagnostics contains no errors then the returned plan is
   149→// applyable, although Terraform cannot guarantee that applying it will fully
   150→// succeed. If the returned diagnostics contains errors but this method
   151→// still returns a non-nil Plan then the plan describes the subset of actions
   152→// planned so far, which is not safe to apply but could potentially be used
   153→// by the UI layer to give extra context to support understanding of the
   154→// returned error messages.
   155→func (c *Context) Plan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   156→	plan, _, diags := c.PlanAndEval(config, prevRunState, opts)
   157→	return plan, diags
   158→}
   159→
   160→// PlanAndEval is like [Context.Plan] except that it additionally makes a
   161→// best effort to return a [lang.Scope] which can evaluate expressions in the
   162→// root module based on the content of the generated plan.
   163→//
   164→// The scope will be nil if the planning process doesn't complete successfully
   165→// enough to produce a valid evaluation scope. If the returned plan is nil
   166→// then the scope will always be nil, but it's also possible for the scope
   167→// to be nil even when the plan isn't, if the plan is not complete enough for
   168→// the evaluation scope to produce consistent results.
   169→func (c *Context) PlanAndEval(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   170→	defer c.acquireRun("plan")()
   171→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   172→
   173→	// Save the downstream functions from needing to deal with these broken situations.
   174→	// No real callers should rely on these, but we have a bunch of old and
   175→	// sloppy tests that don't always populate arguments properly.
   176→	if config == nil {
   177→		config = configs.NewEmptyConfig()
   178→	}
   179→	if prevRunState == nil {
   180→		prevRunState = states.NewState()
   181→	}
   182→	if opts == nil {
   183→		opts = &PlanOpts{
   184→			Mode: plans.NormalMode,
   185→		}
   186→	}
   187→
   188→	moreDiags := c.checkConfigDependencies(config)
   189→	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   190→	moreDiags = c.checkStateDependencies(prevRunState)
   191→	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   192→
   193→	// If required dependencies are not available then we'll bail early since
   194→	// otherwise we're likely to just see a bunch of other errors related to
   195→	// incompatibilities, which could be overwhelming for the user.
   196→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   197→		return nil, nil, diags
   198→	}
   199→
   200→	providerCfgDiags := checkExternalProviders(config, nil, prevRunState, opts.ExternalProviders)
   201→	diags = diags.Append(providerCfgDiags)
   202→	if providerCfgDiags.HasErrors() {
   203→		return nil, nil, diags
   204→	}
   205→
   206→	switch opts.Mode {
   207→	case plans.NormalMode, plans.DestroyMode:
   208→		// OK
   209→	case plans.RefreshOnlyMode:
   210→		if opts.SkipRefresh {
   211→			// The CLI layer (and other similar callers) should prevent this
   212→			// combination of options.
   213→			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   214→				tfdiags.Error,
   215→				"Incompatible plan options",
   216→				"Cannot skip refreshing in refresh-only mode. This is a bug in Terraform.",
   217→			))
   218→			return nil, nil, diags
   219→		}
   220→	default:
   221→		// The CLI layer (and other similar callers) should not try to
   222→		// create a context for a mode that Terraform Core doesn't support.
   223→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   224→			tfdiags.Error,
   225→			"Unsupported plan mode",
   226→			fmt.Sprintf("Terraform Core doesn't know how to handle plan mode %s. This is a bug in Terraform.", opts.Mode),
   227→		))
   228→		return nil, nil, diags
   229→	}
   230→	if len(opts.ForceReplace) > 0 && opts.Mode != plans.NormalMode {
   231→		// The other modes don't generate no-op or update actions that we might
   232→		// upgrade to be "replace", so doesn't make sense to combine those.
   233→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   234→			tfdiags.Error,
   235→			"Unsupported plan mode",
   236→			"Forcing resource instance replacement (with -replace=...) is allowed only in normal planning mode.",
   237→		))
   238→		return nil, nil, diags
   239→	}
   240→	if opts.Forget && opts.Mode != plans.DestroyMode {
   241→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   242→			tfdiags.Error,
   243→			"Unsupported plan mode",
   244→			"Forgetting all resources is only allowed in the context of a destroy plan. This is a bug in Terraform, please report it.",
   245→		))
   246→		return nil, nil, diags
   247→	}
   248→
   249→	// By the time we get here, we should have values defined for all of
   250→	// the root module variables, even if some of them are "unknown". It's the
   251→	// caller's responsibility to have already handled the decoding of these
   252→	// from the various ways the CLI allows them to be set and to produce
   253→	// user-friendly error messages if they are not all present, and so
   254→	// the error message from checkInputVariables should never be seen and
   255→	// includes language asking the user to report a bug.
   256→	varDiags := checkInputVariables(config.Module.Variables, opts.SetVariables)
   257→	diags = diags.Append(varDiags)
   258→
   259→	if len(opts.Targets) > 0 {
   260→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   261→			tfdiags.Warning,
   262→			"Resource targeting is in effect",
   263→			`You are creating a plan with the -target option, which means that the result of this plan may not represent all of the changes requested by the current configuration.
   264→
   265→The -target option is not for routine use, and is provided only for exceptional situations such as recovering from errors or mistakes, or when Terraform specifically suggests to use it as part of an error message.`,
   266→		))
   267→	}
   268→
   269→	var plan *plans.Plan
   270→	var planDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   271→	var evalScope *lang.Scope
   272→	switch opts.Mode {
   273→	case plans.NormalMode:
   274→		plan, evalScope, planDiags = c.plan(config, prevRunState, opts)
   275→	case plans.DestroyMode:
   276→		plan, evalScope, planDiags = c.destroyPlan(config, prevRunState, opts)
   277→	case plans.RefreshOnlyMode:
   278→		plan, evalScope, planDiags = c.refreshOnlyPlan(config, prevRunState, opts)
   279→	default:
   280→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("unsupported plan mode %s", opts.Mode))
   281→	}
   282→	diags = diags.Append(planDiags)
   283→	// NOTE: We're intentionally not returning early when diags.HasErrors
   284→	// here because we'll still populate other metadata below on a best-effort
   285→	// basis to try to give the UI some extra context to return alongside the
   286→	// error messages.
   287→

   401→//
   402→// The "mode" and "setVariables" arguments become the values of the "Mode"
   403→// and "SetVariables" fields in the result. Refer to the PlanOpts type

   412→func (c *Context) plan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   413→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   414→	if opts.Mode != plans.NormalMode {
   415→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("called Context.plan with %s", opts.Mode))
   416→	}
   417→
   418→	plan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, prevRunState, opts)
   419→	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   420→
   421→	return plan, evalScope, diags
   422→}

   424→func (c *Context) refreshOnlyPlan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   425→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   426→
   427→	if opts.Mode != plans.RefreshOnlyMode {
   428→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("called Context.refreshOnlyPlan with %s", opts.Mode))
   429→	}
   430→
   431→	plan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, prevRunState, opts)
   432→	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   433→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   434→		// Non-nil plan along with errors indicates a non-applyable partial
   435→		// plan that's only suitable to be shown to the user as extra context
   436→		// to help understand the errors.
   437→		return plan, evalScope, diags
   438→	}
   439→
   440→	// If the graph builder and graph nodes correctly obeyed our directive
   441→	// to refresh only, the set of resource changes should always be empty.
   442→	// We'll safety-check that here so we can return a clear message about it,
   443→	// rather than probably just generating confusing output at the UI layer.
   444→	if len(plan.Changes.Resources) != 0 {
   445→		// Some extra context in the logs in case the user reports this message
   446→		// as a bug, as a starting point for debugging.
   447→		for _, rc := range plan.Changes.Resources {
   448→			if depKey := rc.DeposedKey; depKey == states.NotDeposed {
   449→				log.Printf("[DEBUG] Refresh-only plan includes %s change for %s", rc.Action, rc.Addr)
   450→			} else {
   451→				log.Printf("[DEBUG] Refresh-only plan includes %s change for %s deposed object %s", rc.Action, rc.Addr, depKey)
   452→			}
   453→		}
   454→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   455→			tfdiags.Error,
   456→			"Invalid refresh-only plan",
   457→			"Terraform generated planned resource changes in a refresh-only plan. This is a bug in Terraform.",
   458→		))
   459→	}
   460→
   461→	// We don't populate RelevantResources for a refresh-only plan, because
   462→	// they never have any planned actions and so no resource can ever be
   463→	// "relevant" per the intended meaning of that field.
   464→
   465→	return plan, evalScope, diags
   466→}

   468→func (c *Context) destroyPlan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   469→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   470→
   471→	if opts.Mode != plans.DestroyMode {
   472→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("called Context.destroyPlan with %s", opts.Mode))
   473→	}
   474→
   475→	priorState := prevRunState
   476→
   477→	// A destroy plan starts by running Refresh to read any pending data
   478→	// sources, and remove missing managed resources. This is required because
   479→	// a "destroy plan" is only creating delete changes, and is essentially a
   480→	// local operation.
   481→	//
   482→	// NOTE: if skipRefresh _is_ set then we'll rely on the destroy-plan walk
   483→	// below to upgrade the prevRunState and priorState both to the latest
   484→	// resource type schemas, so NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance.Execute
   485→	// must coordinate with this by taking that action only when c.skipRefresh
   486→	// _is_ set. This coupling between the two is unfortunate but necessary
   487→	// to work within our current structure.
   488→	if !opts.SkipRefresh && !prevRunState.Empty() {
   489→		log.Printf("[TRACE] Context.destroyPlan: calling Context.plan to get the effect of refreshing the prior state")
   490→		refreshOpts := *opts
   491→		refreshOpts.Mode = plans.NormalMode
   492→		refreshOpts.PreDestroyRefresh = true
   493→
   494→		// FIXME: A normal plan is required here to refresh the state, because
   495→		// the state and configuration may not match during a destroy, and a
   496→		// normal refresh plan can fail with evaluation errors. In the future
   497→		// the destroy plan should take care of refreshing instances itself,
   498→		// where the special cases of evaluation and skipping condition checks
   499→		// can be done.
   500→		refreshPlan, _, refreshDiags := c.plan(config, prevRunState, &refreshOpts)
   501→		if refreshDiags.HasErrors() {
   502→			// NOTE: Normally we'd append diagnostics regardless of whether
   503→			// there are errors, just in case there are warnings we'd want to
   504→			// preserve, but we're intentionally _not_ doing that here because
   505→			// if the first plan succeeded then we'll be running another plan
   506→			// in DestroyMode below, and we don't want to double-up any
   507→			// warnings that both plan walks would generate.
   508→			// (This does mean we won't show any warnings that would've been
   509→			// unique to only this walk, but we're assuming here that if the
   510→			// warnings aren't also applicable to a destroy plan then we'd
   511→			// rather not show them here, because this non-destroy plan for
   512→			// refreshing is largely an implementation detail.)
   513→			diags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)
   514→			return nil, nil, diags
   515→		}
   516→
   517→		// We'll use the refreshed state -- which is the  "prior state" from
   518→		// the perspective of this "destroy plan" -- as the starting state
   519→		// for our destroy-plan walk, so it can take into account if we
   520→		// detected during refreshing that anything was already deleted outside
   521→		// of Terraform.
   522→		priorState = refreshPlan.PriorState.DeepCopy()
   523→
   524→		// The refresh plan may have upgraded state for some resources, make
   525→		// sure we store the new version.
   526→		prevRunState = refreshPlan.PrevRunState.DeepCopy()
   527→		log.Printf("[TRACE] Context.destroyPlan: now _really_ creating a destroy plan")
   528→	}
   529→
   530→	destroyPlan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, priorState, opts)
   531→	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   532→	if walkDiags.HasErrors() {
   533→		// Non-nil plan along with errors indicates a non-applyable partial
   534→		// plan that's only suitable to be shown to the user as extra context
   535→		// to help understand the errors.
   536→		return destroyPlan, evalScope, diags
   537→	}
   538→
   539→	if !opts.SkipRefresh {
   540→		// If we didn't skip refreshing then we want the previous run state to
   541→		// be the one we originally fed into the c.refreshOnlyPlan call above,

Refer to the PlanOpts type\n// documentation to learn about the meanings of those fields.\nfunc SimplePlanOpts(mode plans.Mode, setVariables InputValues) *PlanOpts {\n\treturn &PlanOpts{\n\t\tMode:         mode,\n\t\tSetVariables: setVariables,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (c *Context) plan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tif opts.Mode != plans.NormalMode {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"called Context.plan with %s\", opts.Mode))\n\t}\n\n\tplan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, prevRunState, opts)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(walkDiags)\n\n\treturn plan, evalScope, diags\n}\n\nfunc (c *Context) refreshOnlyPlan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tif opts.Mode != plans.RefreshOnlyMode {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"called Context.refreshOnlyPlan with %s\", opts.Mode))\n\t}\n\n\tplan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, prevRunState, opts)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(walkDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t// Non-nil plan along with errors indicates a non-applyable partial\n\t\t// plan that's only suitable to be shown to the user as extra context\n\t\t// to help understand the errors.\n\t\treturn plan, evalScope, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// If the graph builder and graph nodes correctly obeyed our directive\n\t// to refresh only, the set of resource changes should always be empty.\n\t// We'll safety-check that here so we can return a clear message about it,\n\t// rather than probably just generating confusing output at the UI layer.\n\tif len(plan.Changes.Resources) != 0 {\n\t\t// Some extra context in the logs in case the user reports this message\n\t\t// as a bug, as a starting point for debugging.\n\t\tfor _, rc := range plan.Changes.Resources {\n\t\t\tif depKey := rc.DeposedKey; depKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Refresh-only plan includes %s change for %s\", rc.Action, rc.Addr)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Refresh-only plan includes %s change for %s deposed object %s\", rc.Action, rc.Addr, depKey)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Invalid refresh-only plan\",\n\t\t\t\"Terraform generated planned resource changes in a refresh-only plan. This is a bug in Terraform.\",\n\t\t))\n\t}\n\n\t// We don't populate RelevantResources for a refresh-only plan, because\n\t// they never have any planned actions and so no resource can ever be\n\t// \"relevant\" per the intended meaning of that field.\n\n\treturn plan, evalScope, diags\n}\n\nfunc (c *Context) destroyPlan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tif opts.Mode != plans.DestroyMode {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"called Context.destroyPlan with %s\", opts.Mode))\n\t}\n\n\tpriorState := prevRunState\n\n\t// A destroy plan starts by running Refresh to read any pending data\n\t// sources, and remove missing managed resources. This is required because\n\t// a \"destroy plan\" is only creating delete changes, and is essentially a\n\t// local operation.\n\t//\n\t// NOTE: if skipRefresh _is_ set then we'll rely on the destroy-plan walk\n\t// below to upgrade the prevRunState and priorState both to the latest\n\t// resource type schemas, so NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance.Execute\n\t// must coordinate with this by taking that action only when c.skipRefresh\n\t// _is_ set. This coupling between the two is unfortunate but necessary\n\t// to work within our current structure.\n\tif !opts.SkipRefresh && !prevRunState.Empty() {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] Context.destroyPlan: calling Context.plan to get the effect of refreshing the prior state\")\n\t\trefreshOpts := *opts\n\t\trefreshOpts.Mode = plans.NormalMode\n\t\trefreshOpts.PreDestroyRefresh = true\n\n\t\t// FIXME: A normal plan is required here to refresh the state, because\n\t\t// the state and configuration may not match during a destroy, and a\n\t\t// normal refresh plan can fail with evaluation errors. In the future\n\t\t// the destroy plan should take care of refreshing instances itself,\n\t\t// where the special cases of evaluation and skipping condition checks\n\t\t// can be done.\n\t\trefreshPlan, _, refreshDiags := c.plan(config, prevRunState, &refreshOpts)\n\t\tif refreshDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t// NOTE: Normally we'd append diagnostics regardless of whether\n\t\t\t// there are errors, just in case there are warnings we'd want to\n\t\t\t// preserve, but we're intentionally _not_ doing that here because\n\t\t\t// if the first plan succeeded then we'll be running another plan\n\t\t\t// in DestroyMode below, and we don't want to double-up any\n\t\t\t// warnings that both plan walks would generate.\n\t\t\t// (This does mean we won't show any warnings that would've been\n\t\t\t// unique to only this walk, but we're assuming here that if the\n\t\t\t// warnings aren't also applicable to a destroy plan then we'd\n\t\t\t// rather not show them here, because this non-destroy plan for\n\t\t\t// refreshing is largely an implementation detail.)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)\n\t\t\treturn nil, nil, diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// We'll use the refreshed state -- which is the  \"prior state\" from\n\t\t// the perspective of this \"destroy plan\" -- as the starting state\n\t\t// for our destroy-plan walk, so it can take into account if we\n\t\t// detected during refreshing that anything was already deleted outside\n\t\t// of Terraform.\n\t\tpriorState = refreshPlan.PriorState.DeepCopy()\n\n\t\t// The refresh plan may have upgraded state for some resources, make\n\t\t// sure we store the new version.\n\t\tprevRunState = refreshPlan.PrevRunState.DeepCopy()\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] Context.destroyPlan: now _really_ creating a destroy plan\")\n\t}\n\n\tdestroyPlan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, priorState, opts)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(walkDiags)\n\tif walkDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t// Non-nil plan along with errors indicates a non-applyable partial\n\t\t// plan that's only suitable to be shown to the user as extra context\n\t\t// to help understand the errors.\n\t\treturn destroyPlan, evalScope, diags\n\t}\n\n\tif !opts.SkipRefresh {\n\t\t// If we didn't skip refreshing then we want the previous run state to\n\t\t// be the one we originally fed into the c.refreshOnlyPlan call above,\n\t\t// not the refreshed version we used for the destroy planWalk.\n\t\tdestroyPlan.PrevRunState = prevRunState\n\t}\n\n\trelevantAttrs, rDiags := c.relevantResourceAttrsForPlan(config, destroyPlan)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(rDiags)\n\n\tdestroyPlan.RelevantAttributes = relevantAttrs", "numLines": 150, "startLine": 400, "totalLines": 1188}}

     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2"
    11→
    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    13→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"
    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
    15→)
    16→
    17→func buildProviderConfig(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsProviderConfig, config *configs.Provider) hcl.Body {
    18→	var configBody hcl.Body
    19→	if config != nil {
    20→		configBody = config.Config
    21→	}
    22→
    23→	var inputBody hcl.Body
    24→	inputConfig := ctx.ProviderInput(addr)
    25→	if len(inputConfig) > 0 {
    26→		inputBody = configs.SynthBody("<input-prompt>", inputConfig)
    27→	}
    28→
    29→	switch {
    30→	case configBody != nil && inputBody != nil:
    31→		log.Printf("[TRACE] buildProviderConfig for %s: merging explicit config and input", addr)
    32→		return hcl.MergeBodies([]hcl.Body{inputBody, configBody})
    33→	case configBody != nil:
    34→		log.Printf("[TRACE] buildProviderConfig for %s: using explicit config only", addr)
    35→		return configBody
    36→	case inputBody != nil:
    37→		log.Printf("[TRACE] buildProviderConfig for %s: using input only", addr)
    38→		return inputBody
    39→	default:
    40→		log.Printf("[TRACE] buildProviderConfig for %s: no configuration at all", addr)
    41→		return hcl.EmptyBody()
    42→	}
    43→}
    44→
    45→// getProvider returns the providers.Interface and schema for a given provider.
    46→func getProvider(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsProviderConfig) (providers.Interface, providers.ProviderSchema, error) {
    47→	if addr.Provider.Type == "" {
    48→		// Should never happen
    49→		panic("GetProvider used with uninitialized provider configuration address")
    50→	}
    51→	provider := ctx.Provider(addr)
    52→	if provider == nil {
    53→		return nil, providers.ProviderSchema{}, fmt.Errorf("provider %s not initialized", addr)
    54→	}
    55→	// Not all callers require a schema, so we will leave checking for a nil
    56→	// schema to the callers.
    57→	schema, err := ctx.ProviderSchema(addr)
    58→	if err != nil {
    59→		return nil, providers.ProviderSchema{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to read schema for provider %s: %w", addr, err)
    60→	}
    61→	return provider, schema, nil
    62→}
    63→

hcl.EmptyBody()\n\t}\n}\n\n// getProvider returns the providers.Interface and schema for a given provider.\nfunc getProvider(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsProviderConfig) (providers.Interface,     29→// NodePlannableResourceInstance represents a _single_ resource
    30→// instance that is plannable. This means this represents a single
    31→// count index, for example.
    32→type NodePlannableResourceInstance struct {
    33→	*NodeAbstractResourceInstance
    34→	ForceCreateBeforeDestroy bool
    35→
    36→	// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances
    37→	skipRefresh bool
    38→
    39→	// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions
    40→	// for any instances.
    41→	skipPlanChanges bool
    42→
    43→	// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to
    44→	// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so
    45→	// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource
    46→	// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.
    47→	forceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    48→
    49→	// replaceTriggeredBy stores references from replace_triggered_by which
    50→	// triggered this instance to be replaced.
    51→	replaceTriggeredBy []*addrs.Reference
    52→
    53→	// importTarget, if populated, contains the information necessary to plan
    54→	// an import of this resource.
    55→	importTarget cty.Value
    56→}
    57→
    58→var (
    59→	_ GraphNodeModuleInstance       = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    60→	_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    61→	_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    62→	_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    63→	_ GraphNodeResourceInstance     = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    64→	_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    65→	_ GraphNodeAttachResourceState  = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    66→	_ GraphNodeExecutable           = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    67→)
    68→
    69→// GraphNodeEvalable
    70→func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
    71→	addr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()
    72→
    73→	// Eval info is different depending on what kind of resource this is
    74→	switch addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {
    75→	case addrs.ManagedResourceMode:
    76→		return n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)
    77→	case addrs.DataResourceMode:
    78→		return n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)
    79→	case addrs.EphemeralResourceMode:
    80→		return n.ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx)
    81→	default:
    82→		panic(fmt.Errorf("unsupported resource mode %s", n.Config.Mode))
    83→	}
    84→}
    85→
    86→func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
    87→	config := n.Config
    88→	addr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()
    89→
    90→	var change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange
    91→
    92→	_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)
    93→	diags = diags.Append(err)
    94→	if diags.HasErrors() {
    95→		return diags
    96→	}
    97→
    98→	diags = diags.Append(validateSelfRef(addr.Resource, config.Config, providerSchema))
    99→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   100→		return diags
   101→	}
   102→
   103→	checkRuleSeverity := tfdiags.Error
   104→	if n.skipPlanChanges || n.preDestroyRefresh {
   105→		checkRuleSeverity = tfdiags.Warning
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	deferrals := ctx.Deferrals()
   109→	change, state, deferred, repeatData, planDiags := n.planDataSource(ctx, checkRuleSeverity, n.skipPlanChanges, deferrals.ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(addr, n.Dependencies))
   110→	diags = diags.Append(planDiags)
   111→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   112→		return diags
   113→	}
   114→
   115→	// A nil change here indicates that Terraform is deciding NOT to make a
   116→	// change at all. In which case even if we wanted to try and defer it
   117→	// (because of a dependency) we can't as there is no change to defer.
   118→	//
   119→	// The most common case for this is when the data source is being refreshed
   120→	// but depends on unknown values or dependencies which means we just skip
   121→	// refreshing the data source. We maintain that behaviour here.
   122→	if change != nil && deferred != nil {
   123→		// Then this data source got deferred by the provider during planning.
   124→		deferrals.ReportDataSourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, change)
   125→	} else {
   126→		// Not deferred; business as usual.
   127→
   128→		// write the data source into both the refresh state and the
   129→		// working state
   130→		diags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, refreshState))
   131→		if diags.HasErrors() {
   132→			return diags
   133→		}
   134→		diags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState))
   135→		if diags.HasErrors() {
   136→			return diags
   137→		}
   138→
   139→		diags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, ""))
   140→
   141→		// Post-conditions might block further progress. We intentionally do this
   142→		// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against
   143→		// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations
   144→		// until the user makes the condition succeed.
   145→		checkDiags := evalCheckRules(
   146→			addrs.ResourcePostcondition,
   147→			n.Config.Postconditions,
   148→			ctx, addr, repeatData,
   149→			checkRuleSeverity,
   150→		)
